identity.name: FlowState Builder
identity.description: Dark mode for the 'Editor Interface' to recede and focus attention. Light mode for the 'Canvas' to represent the blank page.

headings.usage: Sidebar headers, Toolbar titles, Modal headings

body.usage: Labels, inputs, list items, help text

mono.usage: Code snippets, pixel values, technical data

sidebar.content: Draggable Elements (Text, Image, Container, Button, Form), Pages, Layers

toolbar.content: Device Toggles, Undo/Redo, Zoom, Preview, Publish

canvas_area.behavior: Infinite scroll or fixed page dimensions, Center aligned

properties_panel.behavior: Context-sensitive. Shows properties for selected element. Hidden when nothing selected.

drag_and_drop.mandate: MUST use dnd-kit for all drag and drop interactions. Do not use react-dnd or html5 native dnd directly.

ui_elements.buttons: Shadcn Button (customized: pill shape for primary actions, ghost for toolbars)
ui_elements.inputs: Shadcn Input (dark mode variant: bg-neutral-800 border-neutral-700)

icons.style: Stroke width 1.5px for UI, 2px for canvas elements

instructions_to_main_agent: 2. SCAFFOLD THE EDITOR: Create a layout with `Sidebar` (left), `Toolbar` (top), and `Canvas` (center). Use `dnd-kit`'s `DndContext` at the root of the builder.
3. IMPLEMENT THE CANVAS: The canvas should be a `Droppable` area. Elements inside should be `Sortable`.
4. AI INTEGRATION: The AI prompt should generate a JSON structure representing the page (sections -> elements). This JSON is the 'state' of the builder.

5. MOCKED AI RESPONSE: Since we don't have the real GPT-5.2 backend connected yet, create a MOCK function that returns a complex JSON object with 4-5 sections (Hero, Features, Testimonials, CTA, Footer) populated with the stock images provided above.
6. EXPORT: The 'Publish' button should simply `JSON.stringify` the current state and trigger a file download.
7. STYLING: Use the dark theme colors for the editor UI. Ensure the canvas stands out as a 'sheet of paper'.
8. DRAG OVERLAY: Implement a `DragOverlay` so users see what they are dragging clearly.
UNIVERSAL_GUIDELINES_FOR_MAIN_AGENT: - Dark colors look good when used independently without gradients. DO NOT use dark colors as gradients
- Don't make generic centered layouts, simplistic gradients, and uniform styling.
- Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy
- Use glass-morphism effects with backdrop filters (12-24px blur)
- You MUST NOT apply universal transition. Eg: `transition: all`. This results in breaking transforms. Always add transitions for specific interactive elements like button, input excluding transforms
- You MUST NOT center align the app container, ie do not add `.App { text-align: center; }` in the css file. This disrupts the human natural reading flow of text
- Always use modern button styles like pill-shaped, or sharp button with interaction animations & color relevant to the app style
- NEVER: use AI assistant Emoji characters like 🤖🧠 etc for icons.
- Component Reuse: Prioritize using pre-existing components from src/components/ui when applicable.
